topic.name: Video Game Development and Genres
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of video game development, including specific games, their genres, and the contributions of developers.

1: Sonic the Hedgehog 2 is a platform video game developed by Sonic Team.
The game is the second main entry in the Sonic the Hedgehog series.
2: Braid is a platform and puzzle video game developed by Number None, Inc.
The game features traditional aspects of the platform genre while integrating various powers of time-manipulation.
3: SSX 3 is a snowboard racing game developed by EA Canada.
The game was initially released on October 20, 2003 for the GameCube, PlayStation 2, and Xbox.
5: Mario Kart DS is a go-kart racing game developed and published by Nintendo.
The game is the fifth installment in the Mario Kart series of video games.
4: First-person shooter (FPS) is a video game genre centered on gun and projectile weapon-based combat through a first-person perspective.
The first-person shooter shares common traits with other shooter games.
6: Alex Seropian is an American video game developer, one of the initial founders and later president of Bungie Software Products Corporation.
Bungie grew to become the best-known Apple Macintosh game developer before being bought by Microsoft in 2001.
7: Europa Barbarorum is a modification of the PC game Rome: Total War based on the desire to provide players with a more historically accurate game experience.
The player controls an empire with the goal of conquering as much territory as possible.
8: Chrono Trigger: Crimson Echoes is a fangame developed as a ROM hack of Square's role-playing video game Chrono Trigger.
It was conceived as an unofficial installment in the Chrono series.
9: Chrono Resurrection is an unreleased fangame developed based on the critically acclaimed Super NES role-playing game Chrono Trigger.
The project was initially called CT64 and was meant to be a complete remake of the original game for the Nintendo 64.
10: Gunfright is an arcade adventure video game developed by Ultimate Play the Game.
In the game, the player takes the role of a sheriff in the town of Black Rock.

topic.name: Cricket History and Players
topic.description: The documents discuss the history and notable players of cricket, focusing on various cricketers' careers and contributions to the sport.

Document 1: Somerset County Cricket Club made their debut in first-class cricket in the 1882 English cricket season.
Wisden Cricketers ' Almanack described the season as 'disastrous'.
Document 2: Stan McCabe was an Australian cricketer who played 39 Test matches for Australia from 1930 to 1938.
He was one of the Wisden Cricketers of the Year in 1935.
Document 3: Wilton H. St Hill was a West Indian cricketer who played in West Indies ' first Test match.
He was highly regarded in Trinidad.
Document 4: Ray Harvey is a former Australian first-class cricketer who played for Victoria in the 1940s and 1950s.
He was born into a working-class, devoutly Methodist family.
Document 5: Irfan Pathan is an Indian cricketer who made his debut for India in the 2003/04 Border-Gavaskar Trophy.
He was instrumental in India 's One-day international and Test series wins in Pakistan in 2004.
Document 6: Alyssa Healy is a cricketer who plays for New South Wales and the Australian women 's team.
She made her international debut in February 2010.
Document 7: Mervyn Roye Harvey was a cricketer who played in one Test match for Australia in 1947.
He was an opening batsman known for his attacking style.
Document 8: Raymond Russell Lindwall was a cricketer who represented Australia in 61 Tests from 1946 to 1960.
He is widely regarded as one of the greatest fast bowlers of all time.
Document 9: Don Tallon was a key member of Donald Bradman 's famous Australian cricket team tour of England in 1948.
This unprecedented feat by a Test side touring England earned them the sobriquet The Invincibles.
Document 10: Douglas Thomas Ring was an Australian cricketer who played for Victoria and Australia in 13 Tests from 1948 to 1953.
He topped the Victorian Cricket Association 's second-grade bowling averages.

topic.name: Professional Wrestling History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the evolution, key events, and notable figures in professional wrestling, particularly focusing on promotions like WWE and NJPW.

1: Sable is primarily known for her time in WWE.
She became the second WWF Women's Champion after the title was reinstated into the company.
2: Mike Mondo is best known for his time as Spirit Squad member Mikey in WWE.
He won the NYWC Heavyweight Championship on two occasions between 2003 and 2005.
3: John Morrison and The Miz was a professional wrestling tag team in WWE.
They first began teaming together in 2007.
4: The Bullet Club is a professional wrestling group, primarily appearing in NJPW.
The group was formed in May 2013.
5: Elimination Chamber (2010) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by WWE.
It was the first event in the Elimination Chamber series.
6: UFC 94 was a mixed martial arts pay-per-view event promoted by the UFC.
The main event featured the UFC Welterweight Champion defending his title.
7: Kia Stevens is best known for her time with TNA Wrestling under the ring name Awesome Kong.
She is a five-time Women's Champion.
8: SummerSlam (1990) was the third annual SummerSlam professional wrestling pay-per-view produced by WWF.
The card featured ten televised matches, including two main events.
9: Over the Limit (2010) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by WWE.
It was the first event promoted under the Over the Limit name.
10: WrestleMania III was the third annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by WWF.
The event is considered to be the pinnacle of the 1980s wrestling boom.

topic.name: Professional Sports and Athletes
topic.description: This cluster discusses notable figures and organizations in various professional sports, including basketball and baseball.

Document 1: LeBron James is an American professional basketball player who is a free agent.
James has won three NBA championships and four NBA Most Valuable Player Awards.
Document 2: Major League Baseball (MLB) is a professional baseball organization, the oldest of the four major professional sports leagues in the United States and Canada.
The organization also oversees Minor League Baseball, which comprises about 240 teams affiliated with the Major League clubs.
Document 3: Lauren Jackson is an Australian former professional basketball player who won multiple championships and Olympic medals.
She was a member of the Australian Women’s National Basketball Team and won three silver medals at the Olympics.
Document 4: Paavo Nurmi was a Finnish middle- and long-distance runner who dominated distance running in the early 20th century.
He won nine gold and three silver medals in his twelve events in the Olympic Games.
Document 5: Howard Staunton was an English chess master regarded as the world's strongest player from 1843 to 1851.
He promoted a chess set of clearly distinguishable pieces of standardized shape.
Document 6: Marty Hogan was an Anglo-American right fielder in Major League Baseball who played for the Cincinnati Reds and St. Louis Browns.
After leaving the National League, Hogan moved on to the minor league Indianapolis Hoosiers.
Document 7: Counter Logic Gaming is an American eSports organization that fields teams in various competitive games, including League of Legends.
CLG has won two NA LCS splits and has been a prominent organization in the eSports community.
Document 8: David Falk is an American sports agent primarily working with basketball players in the NBA.
Falk is best known for representing Michael Jordan for the entirety of his career.
Document 9: The Los Angeles Lakers are an American professional basketball team that competes in the NBA.
The Lakers are one of the most successful teams in the history of the NBA, with 16 championships.
Document 10: Red or Black? is a British television game show which involved contestants making choices in a competitive format.
The show was presented by Ant & Dec and was the most expensive game show in television history.

topic.name: The Boat Race History
topic.description: A historical overview of the annual rowing competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge, known as The Boat Race.

Document 1: The 143rd Boat Race between crews from the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge took place on the River Thames on 29 March 1997.
The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge.
Document 2: The 69th Boat Race took place on 30 March 1912 with a re-row on 1 April.
The race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
Document 3: The 96th Boat Race took place on 1 April 1950.
The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
Document 4: The 128th Boat Race took place on 27 March 1982.
The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing competition between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge along the River Thames.
Document 5: The 25th Boat Race between crews from the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge took place on the River Thames on 4 April 1868.
The race was first held in 1829, and since 1845 has taken place on the 4.2-mile (6.8 km) Championship Course on the River Thames.
Document 6: The 121st Boat Race between crews from the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge took place on the River Thames on 29 March 1975.
The rivalry is a major point of honour between the two universities, followed throughout the United Kingdom.
Document 7: The 48th Boat Race took place on 21 March 1891.
The Boat Race is an annual side-by-side rowing race between crews from the Universities of Oxford and Cambridge.
Document 8: The 69th Women 's Boat Race took place on 30 March 2014.
The Women 's Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing competition between eights from Oxford University Women 's Boat Club and the Cambridge University Women 's Boat Club.
Document 9: The 99th Boat Race took place on 28 March 1953.
The race takes place on the 4.2-mile (6.8 km) Championship Course on the River Thames.
Document 10: The 131st Boat Race took place on 6 April 1985.
The rivalry is a major point of honour between the two universities and followed throughout the United Kingdom.

topic.name: State Highways Overview
topic.description: An overview of various state highways across the United States, detailing their routes, historical significance, and connections to other roads.

Document 1: Recreational Road located in Concho County, in the western portion of the U.S. state of Texas.
connects a recreational area on the O.H. Ivie Reservoir to Farm to Market Road 1929.
Document 2: Maryland Route 300 (MD 300) is a state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
the highway runs 13.55 miles from MD 213 in Church Hill east through Sudlersville to the Delaware state line.
Document 3: State Route 285 (SR 285) is a 5.04-mile state highway serving Douglas and Chelan counties.
The highway serves Wenatchee and begins at an interchange with State Route 28 (SR 28) in East Wenatchee.
Document 4: M-24 is a state trunkline highway in the U.S. state of Michigan that extends 75.691 miles through Southeast Michigan.
It starts at an interchange with Interstate 75 (I-75) and ends where it merges with M-25.
Document 5: Ranch Road 1 (RR 1) is a Ranch Road located in Gillespie and Blanco counties.
The highway is approximately 6.6 miles, and begins at U.S. Route 290 (US 290) in Stonewall.
Document 6: M-93 is the designation of a state trunkline highway in the Lower Peninsula of the US state of Michigan.
It links both Camp Grayling and Hartwick Pines State Park with Interstate 75 (I-75).
Document 7: M-42 is a rural state trunkline highway in the U.S. state of Michigan.
It is located in the northwestern Lower Peninsula of the state.
Document 8: M-88 is a state trunkline highway in the U.S. state of Michigan.
It runs between US Highway 31 (US 31) and US 131 / M-66 in the Lower Peninsula.
Document 9: M-94 is a state trunkline in the Upper Peninsula of the US state of Michigan.
It runs for 86.983 miles from K. I. Sawyer to Manistique.
Document 10: Interstate 605 (I-605) is the common designation given by local media outlets to several proposals for a new auxiliary Interstate Highway.
Proposals have been heard from since the 1960s, including highways connecting from I-5 all the way to the Canada – US border.

topic.name: Naval Warships of World War I
topic.description: The documents discuss various classes of naval warships and their roles during World War I, focusing on battleships and battlecruisers from different navies.

Document 1: The Gangut-class battleships were the first dreadnoughts begun for the Imperial Russian Navy before World War I.
Their role was to defend the mouth of the Gulf of Finland against the Germans.
Document 2: The Indefatigable class were the second class built of British battlecruisers which served in the Royal Navy during World War I.
They spent most of the war patrolling the North Sea, and participated in most of the battles there.
Document 3: The Renown class comprised a pair of battlecruisers built during the First World War for the Royal Navy.
Repulse was the only ship of her class to see combat in the First World War.
Document 4: Sissoi Veliky was a pre-dreadnought battleship built for the Imperial Russian Navy in the 1890s.
The ship participated in the naval blockade of Crete during the Greco-Turkish War.
Document 5: Admiral Graf Spee was a Deutschland-class armored ship which served with the Kriegsmarine during World War II.
The ship conducted five non-intervention patrols during the Spanish Civil War.
Document 6: Scharnhorst was a German capital ship of Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine.
The ship was built at the Kriegsmarinewerft dockyard in Wilhelmshaven.
Document 7: Yakumo was an armored cruiser built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the late 1890s.
She participated in most of the naval battles of the Russo-Japanese War of 1904 – 05.
Document 9: The Majestic class of nine pre-dreadnought battleships were built for the Royal Navy in the mid-1890s.
They were the largest class of battleships in history in terms of the number of member ships.
Document 10: Blücher was the second of five Admiral Hipper-class heavy cruisers of Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine.
The ship led the flotilla of warships into the Oslofjord on the night of 8 April.

topic.description: This cluster discusses various films and notable actors, highlighting their careers, filmography, and contributions to the film industry.

The film tells the story of a paleontologist in a number of predicaments involving a scatterbrained woman and a leopard named Baby.

topic.name: Religious Poetry and Philosophy
topic.description: This cluster encompasses works of religious poetry and theological writings that explore spiritual themes, divine relationships, and the intersection of faith and philosophy.

1: Sonnet 102 is one of the 154 sonnets written by English playwright and poet William Shakespeare.
the poet speaks of a silence between his Muse and himself.
2: Clement of Alexandria was a Christian theologian who taught at the Catechetical School of Alexandria.
Clement was influenced by Hellenistic philosophy to a greater extent than any other Christian thinker of his time.
3: Angelus Silesius was a German Catholic priest and physician, known as a mystic and religious poet.
He composed 55 tracts and pamphlets condemning Protestantism.
4: Thomas Traherne was an English poet, clergyman, theologian, and religious writer.
The work for which Traherne is best known today is the Centuries of Meditations, a collection of short paragraphs in which he reflects on Christian life and ministry.

topic.name: Popular Music Hits
topic.description: The documents discuss various popular music hits and their impact on artists' careers and the music industry.

1: 'Bidi Bidi Bom Bom' was a bridge to the Tejano market.
The single peaked at number one on the United States Billboard Hot Latin Songs chart.
2: I Am ... Sasha Fierce debuted at number one on the Billboard.
The album was formatted as a dual disc, intending to market Beyoncé's contrasting facets of artistry.
3: Glitter was met with generally negative reviews from music critics.
The album was a complete musical departure from any of Carey's previous releases.
4: 'Die Another Day' received mixed responses from music critics.
MGM wanted a high-profile artist for the theme of Die Another Day and Madonna was their choice.
5: 'Dreamlover' helped Carey's transition into the pop music market.
The song received generally positive reviews from contemporary music critics.
6: 'Give Me All Your Luvin'' is a dance-pop song, with elements of new wave and disco.

The single reached number one in fifteen different countries.
8: 'Stronger' became her third song to top the Billboard Hot 100 chart for three non-consecutive weeks.
The song explores themes of empowerment and recovery following a heartbreak.
9: 'Not Afraid' carries a positive message and depicts Eminem's change in direction from drugs and violence.
The song received mixed to positive reviews from music critics.
10: Aphrodite is primarily a dance-pop and disco record.
The album was met with generally positive reviews from music critics.

topic.name: Colonial Administration and Slavery
topic.description: The documents explore the themes of colonial governance, the impact of slavery, and the diverse historical developments in various regions of North America and the Caribbean.

Document 1: Edmund Andros was an English colonial administrator in North America.
His tenure in New England was authoritarian and turbulent.
Document 2: Slavery in Haiti has existed since Christopher Columbus arrived on the island in 1492.
The practice was so devastating to the native population that the Spanish began importing African slaves.
Document 3: The Middle Colonies were the most ethnically and religiously diverse British colonies in North America.
Civil unrest in Europe and other colonies saw an influx of immigrants to the Middle Colonies in the 18th century.
Document 4: For a period of over 7000 years, humans have inhabited the Galveston Bay Area.
Spanish and French explorers traveled the area for many years gradually establishing trade with the local natives.
Document 5: James Jesse Strang was an American religious leader, politician, and self-proclaimed monarch.
He founded the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter Day Saints (Strangite).
Document 6: The history of human activity in Indiana began with migratory tribes of Native Americans.
The region entered recorded history in the 1670s when the first Europeans came to Indiana.
Document 7: The Indiana Territory was created by an Act of Congress and signed into law by President John Adams.
The territory originally contained approximately 259,824 square miles of land.
Document 8: George Washington was the first President of the United States and the Commander-in-Chief of the Continental Army during the American Revolutionary War.
His family were wealthy planters who owned tobacco plantations and slaves.
Document 9: Kingsley Plantation is the site of a former estate in Jacksonville, Florida.
The longest span of ownership was under Kingsley and his family, a polygamous and multiracial household.
Document 10: Bartolomé de las Casas was a 16th-century Spanish historian, social reformer and Dominican friar.
He became the first resident Bishop of Chiapas, and the first officially appointed 'Protector of the Indians'.

topic.name: Athletes in Competitive Sports
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the achievements and backgrounds of athletes competing in various sports, including swimming, skiing, athletics, and basketball.

Document 1: Mary Wayte is an American former competition swimmer, two-time Olympic gold medalist, and television sports commentator.
Wayte won eight medals in major international championships, including four golds.
Document 2: Jessica Gallagher is an Australian Paralympic alpine skier, track and field athlete, and tandem cyclist.
She was Australia 's second female Winter Paralympian, and the first Australian woman to win a medal at the Winter Paralympics.
Document 3: Teigan Van Roosmalen is an Australian Paralympic S13 swimmer.
She competed at the 2011 Para Pan Pacific Championships in Edmonton, where she won a gold medal.
Document 5: Jessica Hardy is an American competition swimmer who specializes in breaststroke and freestyle events.
Hardy earned a bronze medal in the 4 × 100-meter freestyle and a gold medal in the 4 × 100-meter medley relays at the 2012 Summer Olympics.
Document 10: Mareike Adermann is a 4.5 point wheelchair basketball player, who plays for the University of Wisconsin-Whitewater.
She won a gold medal at the 2012 Summer Paralympics in London.

topic.name: Naval Warfare History
topic.description: The documents discuss various naval vessels and their roles in military conflicts, primarily during the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

Document 1: HMS Majestic was a Majestic-class pre-dreadnought battleship of the Royal Navy.
She participated in bombardments of Turkish forts and supported the Allied landings at Gallipoli.
Document 2: SMS Seeadler was an unprotected cruiser of the Bussard class, built by the German Kaiserliche Marine.
She participated in the suppression of the Boxer Rebellion in Qing China in 1900.
Document 3: SMS Stuttgart was a Königsberg-class light cruiser of the Kaiserliche Marine.
She saw action at the Battle of Jutland.
Document 4: Berk-i Satvet was a torpedo cruiser of the Ottoman Navy.
She saw action during the Balkan Wars of 1912 – 13 in the Aegean and Black Seas.
Document 5: Empire Endurance was a cargo liner requisitioned by the Kriegsmarine for use as a supply ship.
She was torpedoed and sunk by the German submarine U-73.
Document 6: SMS Rostock was a light cruiser of the Karlsruhe class built by the German Kaiserliche Marine.
She served with the High Seas Fleet as a leader of torpedo boat flotillas.
Document 7: Giuseppe Garibaldi was the lead ship of her class of armored cruisers built for the Royal Italian Navy.
She bombarded Tripoli at the beginning of the Italo-Turkish War of 1911 – 12.
Document 9: Principe di Carignano was the lead ship of the Principe di Carignano class of ironclad warships.
She saw action during the Battle of Lissa in 1866 during the Third Italian War of Independence.
Document 10: HMS Ben-my-Chree was a packet steamer and a Royal Navy seaplane carrier of the First World War.
She was sunk by Turkish artillery while anchored at the recently occupied island of Kastellorizo.

topic.name: Tropical Cyclone Seasons
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical cyclone seasons across different oceanic regions, detailing the formation, intensity, and impact of storms during those periods.

Document 1: 2002 Pacific hurricane season was a slightly above average Pacific hurricane season
three tropical cyclones reach Category 5 intensity

The season began on 1 November 2002 and ended on 30 April 2003
Document 3: 1980 Atlantic hurricane season was tied with 1932, 1969, and 1994 for most named storms
The season officially began on June 1, 1980, and lasted until November 30, 1980

The season began early when an unnamed tropical storm struck Seychelles in September
Document 5: 1990 Pacific hurricane season is the fifth most active season on record

Document 6: 2005 North Indian Ocean cyclone season was destructive and deadly to southern India
The season began early with two systems in January
Document 7: 2001 North Indian Ocean cyclone season was fairly quiet
There were six depressions tracked by the India Meteorological Department
Document 8: 1994 – 95 South-West Indian Ocean cyclone season was fairly active
The first system was Tropical Depression A1, which formed in October
Document 9: 1969 Atlantic hurricane season was the busiest Atlantic hurricane season since 1933
The season officially began on June 1, and lasted until November 30
Document 10: 1975 Pacific hurricane season officially started May 15, 1975
The 1975 Pacific hurricane season was near average, with 17 tropical storms forming

topic.name: Tropical Meteorology and Climatology
topic.description: This topic encompasses the study of tropical cyclones, their formation, and related meteorological phenomena.

1: Matagorda Bay is a large Gulf of Mexico estuary bay on the Texas coast.

2: Hurricane Igor was the most destructive tropical cyclone to strike the Canadian island of Newfoundland on record.
Igor reached Category 4 status on the Saffir – Simpson Hurricane Wind Scale.
3: Tropical cyclogenesis is the development and strengthening of a tropical cyclone in the atmosphere.
There are six main requirements for tropical cyclogenesis.
4: Jet streams are fast flowing, narrow, meandering air currents found in the upper atmosphere.
The strongest jet streams are the polar jets.
5: A low-pressure area is a region where the atmospheric pressure is lower than that of surrounding locations.
The formation process of a low-pressure area is known as cyclogenesis.
9: Hurricane Lili was the second deadliest and second costliest hurricane of the 2002 Atlantic hurricane season.
Lili caused extensive damage through the Caribbean, particularly to crops and poorly built homes.
10: The 2002 North Indian Ocean cyclone season was a below active season in terms of tropical cyclone formation.
Cyclones occurring between the meridians 45 ° E and 100 ° E are included in the season by the IMD.

topic.name: Evolution of Music Genres
topic.description: The documents explore the development and characteristics of various music genres and influential albums from the late 20th century to early 21st century.

Document 1: Dubstep is a genre of electronic dance music that originated in South London, England.
The earliest dubstep releases date back to 1998.
Document 2: The Day the World Gets ' Round is a song by English musician George Harrison, released on his 1973 album Living in the Material World.
The lyrics reflect his disappointment that such a humanitarian aid project was necessary.
Document 3: Watch the Throne is a collaborative studio album by American rappers Jay-Z and Kanye West, released on August 8, 2011.
The album expresses other topics such as Jay-Z's thoughts on fatherhood, West's reflection on being deemed a social villain.
Document 4: Astral Weeks is the second studio album by Northern Irish singer-songwriter Van Morrison.
Blending folk, blues, jazz, and classical music, the album's songs signaled a radical departure from the sound of his previous pop hits.
Document 5: The Book of Taliesyn is the second studio album by English rock band Deep Purple, recorded only three months after Shades of Deep Purple.

Document 6: Let It Down is a song by English musician George Harrison, released on his 1970 triple album All Things Must Pass.
Its brash opening and choruses contrast with the ethereal quality of the verses.
Document 7: All Things Must Pass is a song by English musician George Harrison, issued in November 1970 as the title track to his triple album of the same name.
The subject matter deals with the transient nature of human existence.
Document 8: Wah-Wah is a song by English musician George Harrison, released on his 1970 triple album All Things Must Pass.

Document 10: Garage rock is a raw and energetic style of rock and roll that flourished in the mid-1960s.
The style, a precursor to psychedelic rock, is often characterized by aggressive and unsophisticated lyrics.

topic.name: Diversity in Nature and Culture
topic.description: This topic encompasses the diversity of life forms, ecosystems, and cultural practices as they interact within their environments.

Document 1: Trees are not a taxonomic group but include a variety of plant species that have independently evolved a woody trunk and branches
It is estimated that there are just over 3 trillion mature trees in the world.
Document 2: The Jack Russell Terrier is a small terrier that has its origins in fox hunting
Jack Russells are an energetic breed that rely on a high level of exercise and stimulation.
Document 4: Ecology is the scientific analysis and study of interactions among organisms and their environment
Ecosystems are composed of dynamically interacting parts including organisms, the communities they make up, and the non-living components of their environment.
Document 5: Israeli cuisine comprises local dishes by people native to Israel and dishes brought to Israel by Jews from the Diaspora
Israeli cuisine has adopted, and continues to adapt, elements of various styles of Jewish cuisine.
Document 6: The annelids are a large phylum, with over 17,000 extant species including ragworms, earthworms, and leeches
The species exist in and have adapted to various ecologies - some in marine environments as distinct as tidal zones and hydrothermal vents.
Document 7: The Song dynasty was a culturally rich and sophisticated age for China
Appreciation of art among the gentry class flourished during the Song dynasty, especially in regard to paintings.

topic.name: Political Figures and Movements
topic.description: The documents discuss various political figures and movements across different eras and regions, highlighting their roles and impacts in their respective contexts.

1: Jack F. Matlock, Jr. is an American former ambassador and a specialist in Soviet affairs during the Cold War.
He served as U.S. Ambassador to the Soviet Union from 1987 to 1991.
3: The Armenian Revolutionary Federation (ARF) is an Armenian nationalist and socialist political party founded in 1890.
It campaigns for the recognition of the Armenian Genocide and the right to reparations.
4: Arlen Specter served as United States Senator from Pennsylvania for 30 years.
He was a Democrat from 1951 to 1965, then a Republican until 2009.
6: Babrak Karmal was an Afghan politician installed as president of Afghanistan by the USSR when they invaded in 1979.
He became a leading member of the People's Democratic Party of Afghanistan (PDPA).
8: The Freedom Party of Austria is a right-wing populist political party founded in 1956.
It claims to represent a 'Third Camp' in politics, opposing both traditional left-wing and right-wing parties.
9: The Chris Dodd presidential campaign of 2008 sought the nomination of the Democratic Party for President of the United States.
Dodd's performance in the opinion polls varied as he centered his campaign in Iowa and New Hampshire.
10: Gamal Abdel Nasser was the second President of Egypt, serving from 1956 until his death.
He led the 1952 overthrow of the monarchy and introduced far-reaching land reforms.

topic.name: Military Aircraft Development
topic.description: This topic encompasses the design, development, and operational history of various military aircraft used by different nations, highlighting their roles, technological advancements, and historical significance.

1: Sukhoi Su-35 is a designation for two separate, heavily upgraded derivatives of the Su-27 'Flanker' jet plane.
The first Su-35 prototype made its maiden flight in June 1988.
2: The Supermarine Spitfire was designed as a short-range, high-performance interceptor aircraft.
The Spitfire was built in many variants and was produced in greater numbers than any other British aircraft.
6: The Fairchild Republic A-10 Thunderbolt II is a single seat, twin turbofan engine, straight wing jet aircraft developed for the United States Air Force.
The A-10 was designed for close-in support of ground troops, providing quick-action support for troops against helicopters, vehicles, and ground troops.
9: The Boeing Chinook is a tandem rotor helicopter operated by the Royal Air Force.
RAF Chinooks have seen extensive service including fighting in the Falklands War, peace-keeping commitments in the Balkans, and action in the Iraq and Afghanistan wars.
10: The British Aircraft Corporation TSR-2 was a cancelled Cold War strike and reconnaissance aircraft developed for the Royal Air Force.
The TSR-2 was designed to penetrate a well-defended forward battle area at low altitudes and very high speeds.

topic.name: Baseball Players and Careers
topic.description: The documents discuss the lives and careers of various professional baseball players in Major League Baseball (MLB).

Document 1: Walter Gilbert was an amateur English cricketer who played first-class cricket for Middlesex and Gloucestershire.
He became a professional in 1886 but played only one match before ending his first-class career.
Document 2: Walter Edward 'Jiggs' Parrott was a professional baseball player whose career spanned eight seasons.
He compiled a career batting average of .235 with 174 runs scored in 317 games played.

He is noted for his once promising baseball career, which ended at age 26 because of arm and shoulder injuries.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure Projects
topic.description: This cluster discusses various significant transportation infrastructure projects, including railways and motorways, across different regions.

1: The Follo Line is a planned 22.5-kilometer high-speed railway between Oslo and Ski, Norway.

The network consists of seven lines which radiate from Manchester city centre.

topic.description: The documents discuss notable figures and events related to the nobility and their roles in medieval Europe, focusing on their political power and military actions.

topic.name: World War II Military Commands
topic.description: This topic encompasses the various military commands and units established during World War II, focusing on their formation, responsibilities, and operational history.

1: Western Area Command was one of several geographically based commands raised by the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF) during World War II.
It was primarily responsible for air defence, aerial reconnaissance and protection of the sea lanes within its boundaries.
2: The 7th Infantry Division is an infantry division of the United States Army, best known for its participation in the Pacific Ocean theater of World War II.
It took heavy casualties engaging the Imperial Japanese Army in the Aleutian Islands, Leyte, and Okinawa.
3: The 2/17th Battalion was an infantry battalion of the Australian Army that fought in World War II, participating in significant battles such as Tobruk and El Alamein.
The battalion was involved in campaigns in New Guinea and Borneo during the war.
5: The Long Range Desert Group (LRDG) was a reconnaissance and raiding unit of the British Army during the Second World War.
The LRDG was formed specifically to carry out deep penetration, covert reconnaissance patrols and intelligence missions from behind Italian lines.

topic.name: Legal and Social Activism
topic.description: The documents collectively discuss various aspects of legal frameworks, social movements, and activism related to civil rights, public policy, and individual freedoms.

1: Internal Security Act grants executive power to enforce preventive detention and suppress organized violence.
The landmark case of Chng Suan Tze v. Minister for Home Affairs sought to impose legal limits on preventive detention.
2: Brownie Mary was an American medical cannabis activist known for distributing cannabis brownies to AIDS patients.
Her activism contributed to the legalization of cannabis for medical use in California.
3: A Catholic Statement on Pluralism and Abortion highlighted the conflict between pro-choice Catholics and the Vatican.
It called for value pluralism and discussion within the Church on the subject of abortion.
4: ContactPoint aimed to improve child protection by enhancing information sharing between services.
It faced criticism for privacy, security, and child protection reasons.
5: The Constitution of New Jersey reinforces basic rights and has unique provisions like casino regulations.
It evolved through three constitutions reflecting the state's governance history.
6: The doctrine of legitimate expectation in Singapore protects procedural and substantive rights in administrative law.
It has been recognized by Singapore courts since 2013.
7: Compliance requirements summarize federal laws applicable to federal assistance in the U.S.
They guide recipients in complying with regulations related to federal funding.
8: Antara is Indonesia's national news agency that has navigated its relationship with the government over decades.
It serves as a platform for news dissemination and has faced challenges in maintaining unbiased reporting.
9: The First Amendment protects freedoms related to religion, speech, press, assembly, and petitioning the government.
Its interpretation has evolved through significant Supreme Court rulings over the years.
10: The Weiquan movement in China seeks to protect civil rights through legal activism despite government opposition.
It addresses issues like property rights, environmental damage, and freedom of speech.

topic.name: Science Fiction Films
topic.description: The documents discuss various notable science fiction films, their plots, production details, and thematic elements.

Document 1: Star Trek: Insurrection is a 1998 American science fiction film released by Paramount Pictures.
The crew of the USS Enterprise-E rebel against Starfleet after they discover a conspiracy with a species known as the Son'a.
Document 2: Aliens is a 1986 American military science fiction action horror film written and directed by James Cameron.
The film follows Weaver's character Ellen Ripley as she returns to the planet where her crew encountered the hostile Alien creature.
Document 3: Salt is a 2010 American action thriller film directed by Phillip Noyce, written by Kurt Wimmer, and starring Angelina Jolie.
Jolie plays Evelyn Salt, who is accused of being a Russian sleeper agent and goes on the run to try to clear her name.
Document 4: 2001: A Space Odyssey is a 1968 epic science fiction film produced and directed by Stanley Kubrick.
The film deals with the themes of existentialism, human evolution, technology, artificial intelligence, and extraterrestrial life.
Document 5: Guardians of the Galaxy is a 2014 American superhero film based on the Marvel Comics superhero team of the same name.
The film features an ensemble cast including Chris Pratt, Zoe Saldana, and Dave Bautista.
Document 6: Ra.One is a 2011 Indian science fiction superhero film directed by Anubhav Sinha.
The film follows a game designer who creates a motion sensor-based game in which the antagonist is more powerful than the protagonist.
Document 7: Voices of a Distant Star chronicles a long-distance relationship between two close friends, one a soldier, who attempt to communicate over the course of an interstellar war.
The OVA was adapted into a drama CD and a novel.
Document 8: Hotarubi no Mori e tells the story of a young girl named Hotaru and her friendship with Gin, a strange young man wearing a mask.
Hotaru learns that her friend is supernatural and that touching Gin will cause him to disappear forever.
Document 9: Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind is a Japanese animated epic science fantasy adventure film written and directed by Hayao Miyazaki.
The film tells the story of Nausicaä, the young princess of the Valley of the Wind, who becomes embroiled in a struggle with Tolmekia.
Document 10: Devil Survivor 2: The Animation is a Japanese anime series based on the Nintendo DS video game.
The series stars Hibiki Kuze and his friends as they battle against creatures called Septentriones.

topic.name: Historic Buildings and Architecture
topic.description: The documents discuss various historic buildings and architectural styles, highlighting their significance and cultural heritage.

Document 1: The Masonic Hall in Taunton , Somerset , is designated by Historic England as a Grade II * listed building.
It now hosts nine different Lodges of the Freemasons.
Document 2: The Barrington Civic Center Historic District was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1976.
The district serves as a historically significant example of civic and natural environment planning of the late nineteenth-century.
Document 3: The Grade I listed buildings in Somerset demonstrate the history and diversity of its architecture.
In the United Kingdom , the term listed building refers to a building or other structure officially designated as being of special architectural , historical , or cultural significance.
Document 6: St Dona 's Church , Llanddona is a small 19th-century parish church in the village of Llanddona , in Anglesey , north Wales.
It is a Grade II listed building , a national designation given to 'buildings of special interest , which warrant every effort being made to preserve them.'
Document 7: St James ' Church , Stretham , is an active Anglican church in the village of Stretham , Cambridgeshire , England.
English Heritage assessed the church a Grade II * listed building.
Document 8: Garrick 's Temple to Shakespeare is a small garden folly erected in 1756 on the north bank of the River Thames.
Grade I listed , it was built by the actor David Garrick to honour the playwright William Shakespeare.

topic.name: U.S. and Canadian Highways
topic.description: The documents discuss various U.S. and Canadian highways, detailing their routes, historical significance, and connections to other major roads.

Document 1: U.S. Route 15 is a part of the U.S. Highway System that runs from Walterboro, South Carolina to Painted Post, New York.
The highway connects the county seat of Frederick with Point of Rocks, Leesburg, Virginia, and Charles Town, West Virginia.
Document 2: US Highway 27 is a part of the US Highway System that now runs from Miami, Florida, to Fort Wayne, Indiana.
In the US state of Michigan, it was a state trunkline highway that entered the state south of Kinderhook and ended south of Grayling.
Document 3: Saskatchewan Highway 7 is a major paved undivided provincial highway in the Canadian province of Saskatchewan, running from the Alberta border to Saskatoon.
Highway 7 is an important trade and travel route linking Saskatoon with several of its bedroom communities.
Document 4: Maryland Route 410 is a state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
MD 410 serves as a major east-west commuter route through the inner suburbs on the north side of Washington, D.C.
Document 5: U.S. Route 2 is a component of the United States Numbered Highway System that connects the city of Everett in the U.S. state of Washington to the Upper Peninsula of Michigan.
US 2 forms parts of two National Scenic Byways.
Document 6: State Route 175 is a primary state highway in the U.S. state of Virginia.
SR 175 passes through the northeastern corner of Accomack County, providing the primary access to NASA's Wallops Flight Facility.
Document 7: U.S. Route 412 is a U.S. highway in the south-central portion of the United States, connecting Springer, New Mexico to Columbia, Tennessee.
A 504.11-mile section of the highway crosses the state of Oklahoma.
Document 8: State Route 70 is a state highway in the U.S. state of California.
Connecting Sacramento with U.S. Route 395 near Beckwourth Pass via the Feather River Canyon.
Document 9: King's Highway 7A is a provincially maintained highway in the Canadian province of Ontario that serves as a bypass of Highway 7.
The highway begins in the community of Manchester and travels east through Port Perry, Nestleton Station, Bethany and Cavan.
Document 10: M-18 is a north-south state trunkline highway in the Lower Peninsula of the US state of Michigan.
It runs for 77.530 miles through the central region of the state connecting US Highway 10 near North Bradley with M-72.

topic.name: Scientific Experiments and Theories
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various scientific experiments and theoretical frameworks across multiple disciplines, including communication technology, physics, neuroscience, and philosophy.

1: Speech-generating devices (SGDs) are electronic augmentative and alternative communication (AAC) systems used to supplement or replace speech or writing for individuals with severe speech impairments.
SGDs are important for people who have limited means of interacting verbally, as they allow individuals to become active participants in communication interactions.
2: The impedance analogy is a method of representing a mechanical system by an analogous electrical system.
The mathematical behaviour of the simulated electrical system is identical to the mathematical behaviour of the represented mechanical system.
3: The Fizeau experiment was carried out by Hippolyte Fizeau in 1851 to measure the relative speeds of light in moving water.
His results seemingly supported the partial aether-drag hypothesis of Fresnel, a situation that was disconcerting to most physicists.
4: Slotted lines are used for microwave measurements and consist of a movable probe inserted into a slot in a transmission line.
The parameter most commonly measured by a slotted line is SWR.
5: Power dividers and directional couplers are passive devices used in the field of radio technology.
Directional couplers are most frequently constructed from two coupled transmission lines set close enough together such that energy passing through one is coupled to the other.
6: The decompression of a diver is the reduction in ambient pressure experienced during ascent from depth.
Divers using an atmospheric diving suit do not need to decompress as they are never exposed to high ambient pressure.
7: In neuroscience, long-term potentiation (LTP) is a persistent strengthening of synapses based on recent patterns of activity.
LTP is widely considered one of the major cellular mechanisms that underlies learning and memory.
8: Philosophy of science is a branch of philosophy concerned with the foundations, methods, and implications of science.
There is no consensus among philosophers about many of the central problems concerned with the philosophy of science.
9: Patterns in nature are visible regularities of form found in the natural world.
Natural patterns include symmetries, trees, spirals, meanders, waves, foams, tessellations, cracks and stripes.
10: Quantum electrodynamics (QED) is the relativistic quantum field theory of electrodynamics.
QED mathematically describes all phenomena involving electrically charged particles interacting by means of exchange of photons.

topic.name: Historical Figures and Events
topic.description: The documents discuss various historical figures, their contributions, and significant events from different periods and regions.

1: Jessie Stephen, MBE (19 April 1893 – 12 June 1979) was a twentieth-century British suffragette, labour activist and local councillor.
She became involved in national labour issues as a teenager, via organisations such as the Independent Labour Party and the Women's Social and Political Union.
4: Nothhelm (sometimes Nothelm; died 739) was a medieval Anglo-Saxon Archbishop of Canterbury.
He attended to ecclesiastical matters, including holding church councils.
5: Richard Swinefield (or Richard de Swinfield; died 15 March 1317) was a medieval Bishop of Hereford, England.
He worked to secure the canonisation of Thomas de Cantilupe, his predecessor.

topic.name: Naval Ships and Their History
topic.description: This topic encompasses the design, construction, and operational history of various naval ships from the 19th and early 20th centuries.

Document 1: HMS Royal Oak was a Prince Consort-class armoured frigate built for the Royal Navy in the 1860s.
The ship spent most of her career with the Mediterranean Fleet.
Document 2: SMS Hansa was a protected cruiser of the Victoria Louise class, built for the German Imperial Navy in the 1890s.
Hansa served abroad in the German East Asia Squadron for the first six years of her career.
Document 3: HMS Zubian was a First World War Royal Navy Tribal-class destroyer constructed from the forward end of HMS Zulu.
Zubian saw extensive service in the final two years of the war as part of the Dover Patrol.
Document 4: The French destroyer Panthère was a Chacal-class destroyer built for the French Navy during the 1920s.
She was scuttled when Italy surrendered in September and scrapped after the war.
Document 5: The Briton class was a group of three wooden screw corvettes built for the Royal Navy in the late 1860s.
They were sold in 1886 – 87.
Document 6: Jauréguiberry was a pre-dreadnought battleship of the French Navy, launched in 1893.
She was sold for scrapping in 1934.
Document 7: The Alma-class ironclads were a group of seven wooden-hulled, armored corvettes built for the French Navy in the mid to late 1860s.
Three of the ships participated in the French occupation of Tunisia in 1881.
Document 8: The German Imperial Navy created a series of fleet cruiser designs in 1916 to follow the Cöln-class cruisers.
None of the designs were built, owing to shifting construction priorities in the German Navy.
Document 9: Carnot was an ironclad battleship of the French Navy, laid down in July 1891.
She remained in the French Navy's inventory until 1922.
Document 10: SMS Gazelle was the lead ship of the ten-vessel light cruiser Gazelle class, built by the Imperial German Navy.
She was sold for scrap after the war.

topic.name: North American Fungi
topic.description: This topic encompasses various species of fungi found in North America, detailing their characteristics, habitats, and taxonomic history.

Document 1: Boletellus ananas, commonly known as the pineapple bolete, is a mushroom in the family Boletaceae.
It is distributed in southeastern North America, northeastern South America, Asia, and New Zealand.
Document 2: Wynnea americana, commonly known as moose antlers or rabbit ears, is a species of fungus in the family Sarcoscyphaceae.
It is found from New York to Michigan south to Mexico.
Document 3: Russula brevipes is a species of mushroom commonly known as the short-stemmed russula or the stubby brittlegill.
It is widespread in North America, and was reported from Pakistan in 2006.
Document 4: Aseroe coccinea is a species of stinkhorn fungus in the genus Aseroe.
First reported in Japan in 1989, it was not formally validated as a species until 2007.
Document 5: Suillus quiescens is a pored mushroom of the genus Suillus in the family Suillaceae.
First collected in 2002 on Santa Cruz Island off the coast of California.
Document 6: Coprinopsis variegata, commonly known as the scaly ink cap or the feltscale inky cap, is a species of fungus in the family Psathyrellaceae.
Distributed in eastern North America, it has a medium-sized, bell-shaped to flattened cap.
Document 7: Cyathus striatus, commonly known as the fluted bird's nest, is a common saprobic bird's nest fungus with a widespread distribution throughout temperate regions of the world.
This fungus resembles a miniature bird's nest with numerous tiny 'eggs'.
Document 8: Mycena nargan, commonly known as the Nargan's bonnet, is a species of fungus in the Mycenaceae family.
Reported as a new species in 1995, it is known predominantly from Southern Australia.
Document 9: Mycena atkinsoniana is a species of agaric fungus in the Mycenaceae family.
It is known from the United States and Canada, where it grows scattered or in groups on leaf litter.
Document 10: Mycena cinerella, commonly known as the mealy bonnet, is an inedible species of mushroom in the family Mycenaceae.
It is found in Europe and the United States, where it grows in groups on fallen leaves and needles under pine and Douglas fir.

topic.name: Roller Coaster Designs
topic.description: This topic encompasses the various designs, histories, and experiences of roller coasters manufactured by different companies worldwide.

1: Patriot is a steel inverted roller coaster designed by Bolliger & Mabillard at the Worlds of Fun amusement park
The ride lasts about 2 minutes and 18 seconds and features four inversions
2: SkyLoop is a type of steel roller coaster manufactured by Maurer Söhne
There are currently 10 SkyLoops operating worldwide
3: Nitro is a steel Floorless Coaster at the Adlabs Imagica amusement park
The coaster also features five inversions
6: Nemesis is an inverted roller coaster located at the Alton Towers amusement park
The ride's concept and layout was devised by designer John Wardley
7: Bolliger & Mabillard, abbreviated B & M, is a roller coaster design consultancy
Since 1988, B & M has built 100 roller coasters around the world

topic.name: Video Game Genres and Adaptations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various video games spanning different genres, including platformers, visual novels, role-playing games, and fighting games, as well as their adaptations into other media.

Document 1: Another World is a cinematic platformer action-adventure game
the game influenced a number of other video games and designers
Document 2: Popotan is a Japanese adult visual novel
it has been adapted to other media, including a novel series and anime
Document 3: Mortal Kombat II is a competitive fighting game
the game was an unprecedented commercial success
Document 4: The Mother series is a role-playing video game series
the music has inspired several album releases
Document 5: Planetarian is a Japanese post-apocalyptic visual novel
it was released over the Internet and rated for all ages
Document 6: Ni no Kuni is a series of role-playing games
the games utilize several magic elements
Document 7: Guitar Hero II is a music rhythm game
the game features more than 40 popular licensed songs
Document 8: Dragon Age: Origins is a role-playing video game
the game is played from a third-person perspective
Document 9: Tenjho Tenge is a Japanese manga series
it was adapted into a twenty-four episode anime series
Document 10: Tales of Innocence is an action role-playing video game
the group become involved in the wars consuming the world

topic.name: Historical Landmarks and Geography
topic.description: The documents discuss various historical landmarks and geographical features across different regions, emphasizing their significance and historical context.

Document 1: The Avon Gorge is a 1.5-mile long gorge on the River Avon in Bristol, England.
The gorge forms the boundary between the unitary authorities of North Somerset and Bristol.
Document 2: Flat Holm is a limestone island lying in the Bristol Channel approximately 6 km from Lavernock Point.
It forms part of the City and County of Cardiff and is now managed by Cardiff Council's Flat Holm Project Team.
Document 3: The London Necropolis Railway was opened to carry cadavers and mourners between London and Brookwood Cemetery.
The cemetery was designed to accommodate all the deaths in London for centuries to come.
Document 4: Marrakesh is a major city of the Kingdom of Morocco, known for its historical significance.
The region has been inhabited by Berber farmers since Neolithic times.
Document 5: The Saw Mill River is a 23.5-mile tributary of the Hudson River in Westchester County, New York.
It is the only major stream in southern Westchester County to drain into the Hudson instead of Long Island Sound.
Document 6: Stokesay Castle is a fortified manor house in Stokesay, Shropshire, England.
It was built in the late 13th century by Laurence of Ludlow.
Document 7: Pendennis Castle is an artillery fort constructed by Henry VIII near Falmouth, Cornwall.
It formed part of the King's Device programme to protect against invasion from France.
Document 8: Sandgate Castle is an artillery fort originally constructed by Henry VIII in Sandgate in Kent.
It formed part of the King's Device programme to protect against invasion from France.
Document 9: The Stainforth and Keadby Canal connects the River Don Navigation at Bramwith to the River Trent at Keadby.
It opened in 1802, passing into the control of the River Don Navigation in 1849.
Document 10: Pushkar Lake is a sacred lake of the Hindus located in the town of Pushkar in Rajasthan, India.
It is surrounded by 52 bathing ghats where pilgrims throng in large numbers.

topic.name: Tropical Storms and Hurricanes
topic.description: The documents discuss various tropical storms and hurricanes, detailing their formation, meteorological history, impacts, and damage caused.

1: Tropical Storm Charley was the third named storm of the 1998 Atlantic hurricane season.
Charley 's impacts in Texas and Mexico were locally severe.
2: Tropical Storm Delta was a late-forming tropical storm of the 2005 Atlantic hurricane season.
The storm gradually gained tropical characteristics and was briefly a subtropical storm.
3: Tropical Storm Harvey was a moderately strong tropical storm that caused flooding across south Florida during the 1999 Atlantic hurricane season.
Damage estimates in Florida reached about $ 15 million (1999 USD).
4: Hurricane Rick was the second-latest hurricane ever to make landfall in Mexico.
Damage from Rick was fairly minor.
5: The 1898 Georgia hurricane was the most recent major hurricane to hit the U.S. state of Georgia.
The hurricane caused heavy damage throughout the region, and killed at least 179 people.
6: The 1932 Freeport hurricane was an intense tropical cyclone that primarily affected areas of the Texas coast.
The storm caused heavy rainfall inland, peaking at 9.93 in (252 mm) in Angleton.
7: The 1887 Halloween tropical storm was a late-season tropical cyclone that caused significant damage along the East Coast of the United States.
The storm is best known for the unusually high amount of shipwrecks and maritime incidents it caused.
8: Hurricane Jimena was the tenth named storm and second hurricane of the 2003 Pacific hurricane season.
The weakening storm then crossed the international dateline before dissipating.
9: Hurricane Olivia was considered the worst hurricane to hit Mazatlán, Sinaloa since 1943.
Olivia destroyed 7,000 houses in the region, leaving 30,000 people homeless.
10: The 1864 Atlantic hurricane season was the third consecutive Atlantic hurricane season with no hurricane landfall in the United States.
The actual total could be higher.
